commit.message:
perf: skip unnecessary accumulator writeback in Return handlers

Both the SMI loop and normal dispatch Return handlers were writing back
the accumulator to frame.accumulator before returning. This is unnecessary
because run_inner reads the return value directly from Ok(acc), and the
frame is destroyed afterward (registers released, ICs written back — neither
uses frame.accumulator).

For the SMI loop Return, also skip the intermediate JsValue::Smi(sa)
construction — return Ok(JsValue::Smi(sa)) directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement real-time process lifecycle monitoring via eBPF (#53)

* feat(probes): implement real-time process lifecycle monitoring via eBPF

- Life-cycle tracking: Monitor the full lifespan of a process from creation
  (exec_watch) to destruction (do_group_exit).
- Precise longevity: Capture the exact duration of short-lived processes in
  milliseconds by synchronizing timestamps between exec and exit.
- Contextual linkage: Track cgroup_id to map process lifecycles to specific
  container workloads in Kubernetes/AI training environments.
- Death cause analysis: Enhance exit observability by distinguishing
  between MANUAL_SIGKILL and OOM_TERMINATED through kernel-level victim tagging.
- Reliability: Resolve 'bash' context races and kernel-version dependency
  by implementing name caching and Kprobe-based exit interception.

* lint

* allow dead code in vmlinux structs for CI

- Implement full-path lifecycle tracking (exec → exit)
- Add intelligent OOM vs manual kill discrimination
- Resolve race conditions with process name caching
- Capture precise process duration metrics
- Include cgroup_id for Kubernetes Pod correlation
- Update Helm chart to support process_lifecycle probe

commit.message:
fix(ol-dbt-cli): eliminate false positives in broken_ref_columns and upstream_refs (#2022)

Four root causes of false positive validation errors identified and fixed:

1. Seeds not indexed in manifest.by_name
   - Seeds (resource_type='seed') were stored in registry.nodes but never added to
     registry.by_name, so manifest.get_model() could not find them.
   - Fix: index seeds in by_name alongside models in load_manifest().
   - Effect: upstream_refs no longer warns for ref()s to seeds that have column
     definitions in schema YAMLs (e.g. platforms, user_course_roles,
     legacy_edx_certificate_revision_mapping).

2. UNNEST lateral join columns attributed to upstream ref
   - 'cross join unnest(...) as t(col)' creates an unqualified column 'col'.
   - Pass 2 of get_columns_read_from_ref() skips attribution when a non-passthrough
     regular JOIN is present, but j.find(exp.Table) returns None for an UNNEST node
     so the guard never triggered, wrongly attributing UNNEST aliases to the upstream.
   - Fix: extend the join guard to also fire when j.find(exp.Unnest) is not None.
   - Affected models: int__mitxpro__coursesinprogram, int__mitxpro__coursesfaculty,
     int__mitxpro__programsfaculty, int__ocw__course_departments,
     int__ocw__course_instructors.

3. Unaliased qualified columns from JOIN tables attributed to upstream
   - Pattern: 'select upstream.*, other.col1 from upstream join other'.
   - The passthrough CTE detection collected aliases (exp.Alias) and bare Star nodes
     but skipped unaliased qualified columns (exp.Column with a table qualifier).
     So 'other.col1' was never added to passthrough_added_cols[cte], and downstream
     reads of 'col1' were incorrectly attributed to upstream.
   - Fix: in the passthrough CTE added-column collection loop, capture unaliased
     qualified columns whose qualifier is NOT in passthrough_aliases.
   - Affected models: int__edxorg__mitx_courseruns, edxorg_to_mitxonline_users.

4. Passthrough chain does not propagate added columns across hops
   - Pattern: CTE A (passthrough + adds hash_col), CTE B (select * from CTE A).
   - Pass 1 checked cte_added = passthrough_added_cols.get(table_name) for the
     *immediate* CTE alias, so accessing B.hash_col looked up B's added set which
     didn't include hash_col (only A's added set did).
   - Fix: replace per-CTE cte_added lookup with passthrough_computed (the union of
     all added cols across all passthrough CTEs in the query) for Pass 1 exclusion.
   - Affected model: int__edxorg__mitx_program_certificates.

Tests added for all four patterns in test_impact.py (TestGetColumnsReadFromRef) and
test_validate.py (TestUpstreamRefsSeedResolution).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: inline CreateEmptyArrayLiteral and CreateEmptyObjectLiteral

Inline array and object literal creation directly in the dispatch loop,
avoiding the overhead of the table dispatch function call for these
common operations:

- CreateEmptyArrayLiteral: JsValue::Array(Rc::new(RefCell::new(Vec::new())))
- CreateArrayLiteral: same (dense storage, filled later by StaInArrayLiteral)
- CreateEmptyObjectLiteral: JsValue::PlainObject(Rc::new(RefCell::new(
    PropertyMap::with_capacity(4))))

These are hot operations in array_push_sum, sieve, and object_creation
benchmarks.

perf: allow SMI mode entry on Boolean accumulator with sieve fusion

Major optimization for loops with boolean operations (sieve, if-guarded):

1. JumpLoop now enters SMI mode when accumulator is Boolean (not just Smi).
   Converts Boolean → Smi(0/1) at entry. This enables SMI fast dispatch for
   comparison-terminated loops like \ or(i=0;i<=n;i++) sieve[i]=true;\

2. SMI loop entry now accepts Boolean accumulators defensively.

3. LdaTrue/LdaFalse fused with StaKeyedProperty: the common sieve pattern
   \LdaTrue; StaKeyedProperty arr, idx\ is handled as a single operation
   that writes Boolean directly to the array without leaving SMI mode.

4. LdaKeyedProperty on Boolean array elements fused with
   JumpIfToBooleanFalse/True: the pattern \if(sieve[i]) count++\ stays
   entirely in SMI mode by fusing the boolean load with the conditional jump.

These optimizations keep the sieve benchmark's inner loops fully in SMI mode,
avoiding the costly SMI→normal→SMI dispatch transitions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: share Rc<BytecodeArray> across precompiled benchmark iterations

Each precompiled benchmark was doing Rc::new(bytecode.clone()) per
iteration — heap allocation plus ~15 Rc field clones every time. Now
the BytecodeArray is wrapped in Rc once outside the loop and shared
via Rc::clone (~1 atomic increment per iteration instead of ~16).

This matches real engine behavior where bytecode is compiled once and
shared across invocations, giving a fairer comparison against V8.

commit.message:
perf: rewrite trimDots to avoid repeated string allocations (#8154)

* perf: rewrite trimDots to use index scanning instead of repeated string allocation

The old implementation created a new string on each iteration via substr/slice.
The new implementation scans for the first and last non-dot indices using
charCodeAt, then performs a single substring call. If no trimming is needed,
the original string is returned directly (zero allocations).

Benchmark (20M calls across 10 representative inputs):
- Old: ~628 ms
- New: ~154 ms
- ~4x faster (75% reduction)

Add support for workspace compilation (#2161)

### ❔What, Why & How

<!-- Include description of the changes that will help reviewers in
their task -->

With v28 of BC the ALTool now supports workspace compilation. The key
advantage of workspace compilation is that it supports parallel
compilation which can significantly improve the overall compilation time
for large AL-Go projects.

This PR adds AL-Go support for workspace compilation by adding a new
"Compile" action. Thereby also moving the compilation out of
Run-ALPipeline.

**Limitations**
Limitations that aren't addressed in this PR but are on our radar to fix
in follow-up PRs:
* Support for Directory.packages.props 
* Support for compilation on linux (Status: Bug on Developer
Experiences)
* Support for incremental builds
* Support for compiling BCPT apps
* Support for "Previous Apps" app source validation (i.e. apps from
releases used as baseline)

Related to:
[AB#618073](https://dynamicssmb2.visualstudio.com/1fcb79e7-ab07-432a-a3c6-6cf5a88ba4a5/_workitems/edit/618073)

### ✅ Checklist

- [x] Add tests (E2E, unit tests)
- [x] Update RELEASENOTES.md
- [x] Update documentation (e.g. for new settings or scenarios)
- [x] Add telemetry

commit.message:
perf: extend SMI loop with array access and context slot handlers

Add 6 new opcodes to the SMI inner loop:
- LdaKeyedProperty: Array[Smi] fast path stays in SMI mode when
  element is Smi (helps array_push_sum benchmark)
- StaKeyedProperty: Array[Smi] store with Smi accumulator
- LdaContextSlot/StaContextSlot: closure context access with
  depth-walking, stays in SMI mode for Smi values
- LdaCurrentContextSlot/StaCurrentContextSlot: depth-0 context
  access (common for simple closures like counters)

These opcodes allow loops involving array indexing and closure
variable access to remain in the raw-i32 accumulator fast path,
avoiding JsValue construction overhead on every iteration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add generate-agent skill (#17)

Pure LLM skill that mints a Copilot CLI agent with a distinct personality.
Asks three questions (character, role, scope), then produces two co-located
files: {name}.agent.md (operating instructions) and {name}.soul.md
(personality and identity).

Supports repo scope (.github/agents/) and user scope (~/.copilot/agents/).
Genesis-agnostic — no working-memory or continuity references.
